title: Hidden Columns in Forms
excerpt: We use forms to generate content on Monday boards. Some times we do not want the information entered to be visible once the form is submitted. If we hide the column though it disappears from the form. I would love the ab…

posts.author: betsym (Betsy Minton)
posts.cookedHtml: We use forms to generate content on Monday boards. Some times we do not want the information entered to be visible once the form is submitted. If we hide the column though it disappears from the form. I would love the ability to hide a column so that only board owners can see the information but still have that column appear on the form so information can be added.

posts.author: betsym (Betsy Minton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for the article, but it wasn’t at all what I was asking. I will try to be more clear this time. We have noticed when we hide a column from everyone but the board owners,

that the question related to that column on the form disappears. You can see in the picture below that there is an empty gap where the question should be. If you unhide the column the question will reappear.

Screen Shot 2023-02-13 at 7.45.40 AM1182×910 28.4 KB

There are times when we want to ask questions that perhaps finance or hr needs to know related to an item but we do not want everyone who can view the board to see that information. I would like to be able to hide a column from all but board owners without disappearing from the form. There is already an option to hide a question so it doesn’t make sense that it would have to disappear from the from automatically.

I think this might either be a bug report or a feature request.

Thank you,
Betsy

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @betsym! I hear what you’re saying here. At this time, I am afraid you’d need to hide or set column permissions on the specific column once the form has been submitted as otherwise (as you’ve come to notice) the column will disappear from the form. I will inform the relevant team of your feedback

posts.author: mw01 (MW)
posts.cookedHtml: So they only way around this is to move submitted items from a form to a different board and then set permissions? Seems like such a waste of movement.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t think you’d need to move these items from the board once the form has been submitted unless this is part of your workflow? If the goal is to only have specific information display for specific people once the form is submitted, you’d need to set these permissions after the form is submitted. Alternatively, if you’re on the Enterprise plan you could set item viewing permission in the board and have the relevant people assigned once the item is submitted. Let me know if this makes sense!

posts.author: betsym (Betsy Minton)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have forms that are in continual use that people may submit sensitive information to. We need to question in the form but not visible to everyone with board access.

I think mw01 is right in that a work around may be to have items move automatically to a new board once submitted. Not ideal but its a decent option until the Monday engineers can fix it.

posts.author: aschlukbier (Andrew Schlukbier)
posts.cookedHtml: This seems like a big flaw. If anyone wanted to hide something from the form they can simply hide it in the form view. I can’t think of any good reason for forcing the column to also be hidden from the Form. We have the same problem, as we are looking to have a file submitted by anyone, but that file only be visible to some.

posts.author: Prajwal (Prajwal Kumar)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this feature request, Why such crucial feature is not there in Monday

posts.author: charles.coffey (Charles Coffey)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m having this same issue, and think this should be a reasonable dev update. I want to collect sensitive data via the form, but only want some people to be able to view the columns in the main table. It’s a bit frustrating that it makes the question go away unless you’re one of the people who has the column visibility. I’m surprised more people aren’t facing this issue.

posts.author: jsararas (Jeff)
posts.cookedHtml:
Betsy Minton:

There is already an option to hide a question so it doesn’t make sense that it would have to disappear from the from automatically.

This above is the reason that this should’ve been fixed. We go to the trouble of creating these threads and it’s very frustrating to see months and years pass. How can we submit these things such that they get addressed in a timely manner? This silly [and easily fixable] anti-pattern has just sunk our solution.
